feedback.text: I urge the inclusion of a provision within the Digital Fairness Act to safeguard my ownership of video games. I find the current practice of publishers arbitrarily disabling games I've purchased, is anti-consumer and often specifically designed so the consumer buys the latest version of virtually the same product. Can you imagine owning a phone, and the manufacturer deciding that on a random, not previously agreed to date, it will shutoff the device permanently. The Digital Fairness Act has the potential to establish a landmark precedent for consumer protection by addressing this critical issue. This aligns directly with the "Stop Destroying Videogames" European Citizens' Initiative, which has garnered in excess of 1.4 million signatures, demonstrating widespread public interest and support for reinforcing consumer rights in this domain. I believe it would be highly beneficial for the Digital Fairness Act to affirm my right to continued access and use of software I've purchased. For practical frameworks on how this right could be reasonably implemented, excluding an expectation of perpetual developer support, I respectfully direct your attention to the detailed proposals within the "Stop Destroying Videogames" initiative.
